items.title: TS Compiler Graph MCP: 10x Fewer Tokens in Claude Code and Codex
items.why_it_matters: A new Model Context Protocol (MCP) server maps your TypeScript compiler graph so Claude Code doesn't have to read your entire codebase to understand imports and types. This drastically cuts down token usage and costs when you are letting AI write your Next.js code. You can try this this week by installing the package and adding it to your Claude Code MCP configuration file to instantly lower your token spend on your AI-ingest project.
items.action_test: Install the package and add it to your Claude Code MCP configuration file to instantly lower your token spend on your AI-ingest project.

items.title: Quick Fix: Resolve Claude Code Copy-Paste Terminal Issues with /tui default
items.why_it_matters: If you are using Claude Code in your terminal, a common bug can sometimes break your system's copy-paste functionality within the terminal user interface. Running a simple configuration command resets the terminal state and restores standard clipboard behavior. You can try this this week by typing `/tui default` directly into your Claude Code session the next time your terminal clipboard locks up.
items.action_test: Type `/tui default` directly into your Claude Code session the next time your terminal clipboard locks up.

items.title: Anthropic Releases Claude 3.5 Sonnet V2 / Sonnet 5
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic has updated its flagship model, improving coding logic, instruction-following, and API reliability. Since your stack relies heavily on Claude for both writing code and powering the backend of AI-ingest, this model update directly improves your app's filtering accuracy without changing your code. You can try this this week by updating the model string in your AI-ingest API call to the new Sonnet identifier and running a test batch of tweets through it.
items.action_test: Update the model string in your AI-ingest API call to the new Sonnet identifier and run a test batch of tweets through it.

items.title: Ox: AI Agent That Catches Tech Debt Before You Commit
items.why_it_matters: When you let AI do most of the typing, it's easy to accumulate messy, redundant code that you didn't write yourself. Ox is a lightweight tool designed to analyze your local changes and flag structural issues before you commit them to Git. You can try this this week by running Ox against your local AI-ingest branch before your next commit to see exactly what hidden tech debt your AI assistant generated.
items.action_test: Run Ox against your local AI-ingest branch before your next commit to see exactly what hidden tech debt your AI assistant generated.
